title: Project Manager – Offshore Wind Balance of Plant
description_raw:
Brunel are recruiting for an experienced Project Manager to support the balance-of-plant (BoP) mechanical package delivery on a large Scottish offshore wind development. This is a long-term contract role based primarily in Aberdeen with site visits to the fabrication yard in Nigg.

The BoP mechanical package covers: subsea cable end terminations, J-tube seals, scour protection coordination, TP (transition piece) mechanical interfaces, secondary steel, and boat landing systems.

Role accountabilities:
- Manage the BoP mechanical subcontract package from FEED through to offshore installation and commissioning
- Administer an NEC4 Option C contract with target cost and pain/gain share
- Ensure all technical queries (TQs) and non-conformance reports (NCRs) are processed within agreed SLAs
- Report monthly cost and schedule performance indices (CPI/SPI) to the Programme Director
- Coordinate with offshore transport-and-installation (T&I) contractor during marine warranty process

Profile sought:
- CEng (MIMechE or MICE) preferred, BEng minimum
- 10+ years offshore or marine project management
- NEC3/4 ECC contract administration experience mandatory
- BOSIET/HUET (offshore survival) certificate desirable
- Familiar with DNV or BV class rules for offshore structures

Rate: £800 per day (outside IR35 — engaged via your own limited company or umbrella outside IR35)
Start: ASAP (target mobilisation within 4 weeks)
Duration: 9 months, high likelihood of extension through commissioning phase
Location: Aberdeen city centre office + Nigg fabrication yard visits
